data.title: Adriana Kugler's resignation from the Federal Reserve offers Trump an opportunity to replace Jerome Powell.
data.description: Adriana Kugler, a Federal Reserve governor, unexpectedly resigned, creating an opening for President Donald Trump to select a replacement for Jerome Powell in the future.

data.title: U.S. Tractor Market projected to reach 245,059 units by 2030 from 217,200 units in 2024, growing at CAGR of 2.03%.
data.description: According to a recent report released by ResearchAndMarkets.com, the U.S. Tractor Market was recorded at 217,200 Units in 2024 and is anticipated to grow to 245,059 Units by 2030, indicating a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 2.03%. The U.S. tractor market is highly competitive and is primarily controlled by major players such as John Deere, CNH Industrial, AGCO, and Kubota. These companies collectively hold a substantial share in the market.

data.title: Casella Waste Systems, Inc. is reclaiming $29M worth of Finance Authority of Maine Bonds issued in 2015 and 2018, to be remarketed on August 1, 2025.
data.description: In Rutland, Vermont, on July 17, 2025, Casella Waste Systems, Inc. announced the remarketing of $29.0 million in Finance Authority of Maine Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds. These bonds were initially issued on August 27, 2015, and April 2, 2018, totaling $30.0 million. Casella will redeem $1.0 million of the bonds on August 1, 2025, with the remaining $29.0 million to be remarketed on the same date. Casella is a company that provides regional solid waste, recycling, and resource management services and is listed on NASDAQ under CWST.

data.title: Inflation calm on August 14, 2024; Fed Reserve may start rate-cutting process in September - Lawrence Yun.
data.description: The latest data released on August 14, 2024, reveals that the Consumer Price Index (CPI) indicates a decrease in inflation, suggesting a more stable economic environment. This development signals a potential for the Federal Reserve to initiate a series of interest rate reductions starting in September. Leading economist Lawrence Yun views this change as a positive step towards adjusting monetary policy to stimulate economic growth.

data.title: EHang achieves first pilotless human-carrying eVTOL flight in Africa during Aviation Africa Summit in Rwanda. Milestone for UAM.
data.description: In Guangzhou, China, on September 5, 2025, EHang Holdings Limited (Nasdaq: EH), a leading Urban Air Mobility ("UAM") technology company, made a significant announcement. The company successfully completed the inaugural human-carrying flight of its EH216-S without a pilot in Africa. This milestone was achieved at the 9th Aviation Africa Summit & Exhibition held in Kigali, Rwanda, through a partnership with China Road and Bridge Corporation (“CRBC”). EHang is now the first electric vertical take-off and landing (“eVTOL”) aircraft with no pilot to operate in Africa.

data.title: APAC data center colocation market to grow at 17.25% CAGR from 2024-2030. China, Hong Kong, Australia, Japan, India, Singapore top markets.
data.description: The latest report on the APAC Data Center Colocation Market indicates significant growth potential. The region's market size by investment is projected to increase at a CAGR of 17.25% from 2024 to 2030. Leading countries in this sector include China, Hong Kong, Australia, Japan, India, and Singapore. Additionally, emerging markets such as Malaysia, South Korea, Indonesia, Thailand, and New Zealand are also gaining traction in the APAC region. This information comes from a report that is now available on ResearchAndMarkets.com.

data.title: Tezos (XTZ) price at $0.72 faces resistance at $0.88 amid mixed technical signals. Bulls eyeing momentum.
data.description: Despite showing neutral RSI and bearish MACD signals, the price of XTZ remains steady at $0.72. Tezos bulls are closely watching the key resistance level at $0.88 for a potential momentum shift.

data.title: Jobs report adds just 22,000 jobs in August, likely prompting Fed rate cuts; S&P 500 futures rise.
data.description: The latest jobs report has increased the likelihood of several Federal Reserve rate cuts, as only 22,000 jobs were added in August. S&P 500 futures saw an increase following this news. This development marks a continuation of the trend, as the Fed delivered consecutive rate cuts in response to the previous jobs report. This live coverage highlights the impact of the Jobs Report on the Federal Reserve decisions and the subsequent rise in the S&P 500.

data.title: Tineco showcases smart home products at IFA 2025, focusing on innovation, automation. Highlights include FLOOR ONE S9 Scientist.
data.description: Tineco is showcasing its innovative products at IFA 2025 in Paris, aiming to revolutionize daily life with technology. The company's offerings underline its leadership in the smart home sector, covering areas like scientific precision, steam power, automation, and venturing into new segments like the kitchen. One standout product is the FLOOR ONE S9 Scientist, designed to deliver scientific precision on all surfaces. Tineco's focus on performance and innovation is evident in these exciting developments, setting the stage for a future-focused approach to enhancing daily living experiences.

data.title: Global Enterprises AI market to grow at CAGR of 19.60% from 2024 to 2030. Major players like Microsoft, Google, Amazon Web Services dominate the market.
data.description: A recently published market research report titled "Enterprises AI Market Research Report 2025-2030" projects a significant growth trajectory for the global enterprise artificial intelligence market. It anticipates a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 19.60% from the years 2024 to 2030. The market is characterized by consolidation, with a few key players holding a substantial portion of the market share. Major technology giants including Microsoft, Google, Amazon Web Services, and Meta are leading the charge in this sector.
Japanese Yen increased by 1% to a high of 147 Yen per US dollar during the trading session, reflecting a strengthening in the currency.

data.title: SkyDrive and JR East showcase eVTOL exhibition at Takanawa Gateway City, reach pre-order agreement for one aircraft.
data.description: SkyDrive, a prominent eVTOL aircraft maker from Japan, partnered with East Japan Railway Company (JR East) to host a collaborative exhibition promoting the integration of "railway × eVTOL" travel experiences. This showcase, spanning from August 29 to August 31, highlighted the upcoming possibility for travelers to smoothly merge flights on SkyDrive's innovative electric planes with traditional rail trips. SkyDrive and JR East initiated talks on the potential synergy between their services at this event.
The Bank of America sees two 2025 Fed rate cuts vs none previously.

data.title: ANS Geo and FastGrid merge to create ANS, a national engineering firm with 300+ professionals serving infrastructure, energy, and data centers.
data.description: In Columbus, Ohio, ANS Geo and FastGrid have joined forces to create ANS, a prominent engineering firm comprising over 300 professionals. This new entity will cater to various sectors including infrastructure, energy, and data centers across the country.

data.title: Sarepta Therapeutics awards 25 scholarships for 2025-2026 academic year through Route 79, The Duchenne Scholarship Program.
data.description: Sarepta Therapeutics, a leader in precision genetic medicine for rare diseases, has revealed the 25 recipients of Route 79, The Duchenne Scholarship Program for the 2025-2026 academic year. 20 scholarships will go to individuals with Duchenne, while 5 will be awarded to siblings of Duchenne patients. This marks the eighth year of the program, designed to support those affected by Duchenne muscular dystrophy. The company's announcement did not specify the amount of each scholarship or the total financial commitment for this year.

data.title: Global aftermarket automotive parts market to hit USD 502.61B in 2025 and forecasted to reach USD 756.25B by 2032, growing at a 6.0% CAGR.
data.description: The global Aftermarket Automotive Parts Market is expected to reach USD 502.61 billion in 2025, with a projected growth to USD 756.25 billion by 2032, showcasing a 6.0% compound annual growth rate. The market growth is attributed to the increasing number of vehicles and demand for replacement parts, along with OEMs and distributors focusing on aftermarket networks. Engine components are predicted to be the top-selling product, with passenger cars holding the majority market share. Offline sales channels are anticipated to dominate, with North America leading in market share at 37.6% in 2025. Additionally, Asia Pacific is forecasted to emerge as a lucrative market for aftermarket automotive parts companies. The rising trend of DIY automotive repairs and availability of online repair videos are boosting the demand for individual aftermarket parts, driven by the increasing average age of vehicles, such as in the US where the average age of cars and light trucks reached 12.6 years in 2024. This aging vehicle fleet is expected to fuel the growth of aftermarket automotive parts market due to the need for replacement parts and maintenance services. However, the market growth may be limited by rising preference for OEM products and competition from OEMs.
